vitals.terrain: Mountainous roads through Al Hajar range

course_description.overview: Oman's premier road cycling sportive featuring 240km and 140km routes through the spectacular Al Hajar mountain range to Wadi Dayqah Dam, with stunning climbs, winding descents, and breathtaking landscapes.[1][2]

key_segments.name: Al Hajar Mountains
key_segments.description: Challenging climbs through Oman's rugged mountain range.

key_segments.name: Wadi Dayqah Dam
key_segments.description: Iconic finish at the dam amid raw natural beauty.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: One of Oman's most challenging and scenic cycling events, testing limits with epic climbs and thrilling descents.[1]

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Muscat International Airport (MCT), then transfer to Wadi Dayqah start area approximately 100km southeast.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and resorts in Muscat; camping or lodges near Wadi Dayqah for event weekend.
logistics.registration_details: Registration open now for 2026 event via Muscat Bikes website; secure spot early for 240km or 140km options.[1]
logistics.bike_logistics: Fly with bike to Muscat; local bike shops in Muscat for servicing; road bikes required.
logistics.local_considerations: January weather mild (15-25°C); hydrate for desert climate; Oman visa on arrival for most nationalities; respect local customs.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic road sportive conquering Oman's Al Hajar mountains with unrivaled scenery.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong road cyclists seeking adventure, big climbs, and Middle Eastern cycling prestige.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the unique stunning landscapes and challenge if you crave remote mountain roads.
pro_tips: Register early as spots fill fast.[1]
Train for sustained climbs in heat.
Pack layers for variable mountain weather.
